name: Vercel API
what_it_does: Frontend cloud platform with git-based deployments, edge network, serverless functions, and native Next.js integration for deploying web apps globally.
use_cases: Deploying Next.js applications with zero-config automatic optimization and edge distribution
Running AI-powered edge functions close to users for low-latency inference routing
Managing deployments programmatically via API (create, promote, rollback)
Setting and managing environment variables and project configs via API in CI/CD
Preview deployments per PR branch for automated testing in agent workflows
not_for: Long-running backend services (max 30s serverless timeout on Pro, 60s on Enterprise)
GPU workloads or stateful applications needing persistent compute
Cost-sensitive workloads — Vercel can get expensive at scale vs self-hosted alternatives
best_when: Building Next.js or React apps needing best-in-class developer experience, edge SSR, and tight GitHub integration.
avoid_when: You need more than 60 seconds of execution time, stateful servers, or are cost-optimizing at high traffic volumes.

auth.notes: Access tokens (personal) or OAuth2 tokens for integrations. Tokens scoped to team or user. Deploy tokens available for CI/CD with limited scope.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Hobby: 100GB bandwidth, 100K function invocations/day, 6000 build minutes/month
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro: $20/month/member; Enterprise: custom. Function duration limits increase with tier.
pricing.notes: Bandwidth and function invocations are primary cost drivers at scale. Edge function pricing is separate from serverless function pricing.

performance.rate_limits: API: 3000 requests/minute; deployments: varies by plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API errors return JSON with error.code and error.message; deployment build errors in build log only
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Deployment by git SHA is idempotent; API mutations are not
known_agent_gotchas: Deployment state must be polled — POST /deployments returns immediately, poll /deployments/{id} for READY state
Environment variable changes don't take effect until next deployment — no live reload of env vars
teamId parameter required for all API calls when accessing team projects (not personal account)
Edge runtime vs Node.js runtime have different capabilities — edge has no Node APIs, no file system, limited npm packages
Vercel rewrites and redirects run before serverless functions — misconfigured vercel.json can silently swallow requests

security_score_components.security_notes: Environment variables encrypted at rest and never exposed in logs. Edge config for fast secret reads without cold start.
